workouts.name: B: Upper Pull (Climbing)
workouts.description: Lift B - Climbing-focused pulling. Phase: foundation.
workouts.humanReadable:
UPPER PULL - Climbing focus
Goal: Maximum pulling strength for climbing. This is your highest-priority lift session for climbing performance.

Foundation phase: 3 sets, 8-12 reps, RPE 6-7 (challenging but 2-3 reps in reserve). Focus on PERFECT FORM.

WARM UP (8 min):
  - 5 min light pulling (banded or jumping pull-ups easy)
  - Shoulder CARs x 5 each direction
  - Scap pull-ups x 10
  - Band pull-aparts x 15
  - Easy hangs (bar) 2x 15s

MAIN LIFT:
1. WEIGHTED PULL-UP (or strict bodyweight if not yet weighted): 3x5-8 (unweighted, full ROM)
   - Full ROM: chin clearly over bar, full elbow extension at bottom
   - Climbing's #1 strength predictor

2. ONE-ARM DUMBBELL ROW: 3x10 (moderate weight) EACH
   - Pause at top, control descent. Lock shoulder blade down.

3. INVERTED ROW or TRX ROW: 3x10-12
   - Horizontal pulling balances vertical (pull-up)

4. HANGBOARD PROTOCOL: 5 sets max hang 7s on 20mm edge (start with bodyweight only)
   - 20mm edges, half-crimp or open-hand
   - 90 sec rest between hangs
   - SKIP if fingers feel sore from climbing this week

5. FACE PULL: 3x12-15
   - External rotation + scap retraction = healthy shoulders for high-volume climbing

6. HAMMER CURL (forearm flexor + brachialis): 2x10
   - Forearm endurance for sustained crimping. KEEP this brief - not the goal.

7. WRIST CURL + WRIST EXTENSION: 2x12 each
   - Forearm extensor balance prevents elbow tendinopathy from climbing

8. DEAD HANG (open-hand): 3x max time (target 45s+)
   - Grip endurance + scap stability

Total time: 50-60 min

workouts.name: Rest or Light Climb
workouts.description: Tuesday is your true recovery day. Optional easy climbing only.
workouts.humanReadable:
REST DAY - or optional easy/technical climbing session (60 min, low intensity).

This is your hardest recovery day of the week (between Mon speed and Thu tempo). Use it wisely:
- Sleep in if possible
- If climbing: technical practice, easy slabs, ARC training (NOT projects or limit bouldering)
- Mobility work, foam rolling, hip 90/90s are great
- DO NOT add a run, even an easy one - your legs need this day

Keep HR < 156

workouts.name: A: Lower Body Heavy
workouts.description: Lift A - Posterior chain + eccentric quad. Phase: foundation.
workouts.humanReadable:
LOWER BODY HEAVY - Posterior chain + eccentric quad
Goal: Hip drive for climbing, posterior chain power for running, eccentric quad for downhill (St. George prep)

Foundation phase: 3 sets, 8-12 reps, RPE 6-7 (challenging but 2-3 reps in reserve). Focus on PERFECT FORM.

WARM UP (8-10 min):
  - 5 min easy bike or rowing
  - World's greatest stretch x 5 each side
  - Glute bridge x 15 (activation)
  - Bodyweight squat x 10
  - Cossack squat x 5 each

MAIN LIFT:
1. TRAP BAR DEADLIFT (or Romanian Deadlift if no trap bar): 3x8-10
   - Drive hips back, neutral spine, push floor away
   - Climbing crossover: hip hinge = body tension on the wall

2. BULGARIAN SPLIT SQUAT (DB in each hand): 3x8-10 EACH leg
   - Front foot far enough that knee tracks over ankle
   - 3-second descent, drive up through heel
   - The single best exercise for runners. Don't skip.

3. BARBELL HIP THRUST (or Glute Bridge with band): 3x10-12
   - Pause 1 sec at top, full glute squeeze
   - Posterior chain power = late-race pace maintenance

4. REVERSE LUNGE - SLOW ECCENTRIC: 3x8-10 EACH leg
   - 3-second descent with DBs
   - DIRECT downhill marathon prep - eccentric quad load

5. SINGLE-LEG CALF RAISE (off step): 3x10-12 EACH
   - Slow 3-sec descent, full stretch at bottom
   - Achilles + foot resilience

6. PALLOF PRESS (anti-rotation): 3x10 each side
   - Core resists rotation - critical for running form + climbing tension

Total time: 45-55 min

workouts.name: C: Upper Push + Core
workouts.description: Lift C - Antagonist + stability. Phase: foundation.
workouts.humanReadable:
UPPER PUSH + CORE - Antagonist + stability
Goal: Antagonist work for climbing (prevents overuse injuries), shoulder stability, anti-extension/rotation core.
This is NOT a chest day. We're balancing the pulling-dominant climbing/back work.

Foundation phase: 3 sets, 8-12 reps, RPE 6-7 (challenging but 2-3 reps in reserve). Focus on PERFECT FORM.

WARM UP (5 min):
  - Arm circles + band pass-throughs x 10
  - Cat-cow x 10
  - Push-up to down dog x 8
  - Plank shoulder taps x 10 each

MAIN LIFT:
1. PUSH-UP variations (decline → standard → diamond): 3x8-12
   - Hollow body position, full ROM, controlled tempo
   - Rotate variations week-to-week to keep stimulus varied

2. DUMBBELL OVERHEAD PRESS (standing): 3x8-10
   - Brace core hard, no lumbar extension. This is core work too.
   - Strong overhead press = healthy shoulders for sustained climbing

3. Y-T-W RAISES (light DB or band, prone): 3x8 each letter
   - Mid/lower trap activation - the muscles that protect your shoulders during climbing pulls

4. BANDED PULL-APART: 3x15
   - High volume, light resistance. Pure shoulder health work.

5. PLANK (front): 3x45s
   - Hollow body engagement. Glutes squeezed. NO sagging.

6. SIDE PLANK: 2x30-45s EACH side
   - Lateral core stability for running stride + climbing tension

7. DEAD BUG (slow, controlled): 3x10 EACH side
   - Anti-extension. Lock low back to floor.

8. HOLLOW BODY HOLD: 3x20-30s
   - Total body tension. Climbing's #1 core position. Hands and heels OFF floor.

9. PALLOF PRESS HOLD: 2x20s each side
   - Anti-rotation. Climbing on overhangs requires this.

Total time: 35-45 min

workouts.name: D: Lower Power + Foot
workouts.description: Lift D - Plyometric + injury prevention. Phase: foundation.
workouts.humanReadable:
LOWER POWER + FOOT/STABILITY - Light, plyometric, prevention
Goal: Plyometric power for running economy + foot/calf injury prevention. KEEP THIS LIGHT.
You did your heavy lower work on Wed; this is pure quality movement, not loading.

Foundation phase: 3 sets, 8-12 reps, RPE 6-7 (challenging but 2-3 reps in reserve). Focus on PERFECT FORM.

WARM UP (5 min):
  - Easy jog 5 min OR jump rope
  - Ankle CARs x 10 each direction
  - Calf stretch + Soleus stretch (knee-bent) 30s each
  - A-skips x 20m, B-skips x 20m

MAIN MOVEMENT (POWER):
1. POGO HOPS (low, springy): 3x30s
   - Stay on the ball of foot, minimal ground contact
   - Building tendon stiffness = running economy

2. BOX JUMP DOWN + STICK landing: 2x6 (low box, focus on quiet absorption)

3. SINGLE-LEG BOUNDING (controlled): 2x10 EACH (ONLY if form is clean - skip if fatigued)

PREVENTION & STABILITY:
4. SINGLE-LEG GLUTE BRIDGE: 3x10 EACH leg
   - 1-second pause at top
   - Often-neglected glute med work

5. TIBIALIS RAISE (back to wall, lift toes up): 3x15 EACH leg
   - The anterior shin muscle prevents shin splints. Critical for high-mileage weeks.

6. SINGLE-LEG CALF RAISE (slow, off step): 3x12 EACH
   - 3-sec eccentric. Achilles resilience.

7. TOE YOGA / SHORT FOOT: 2-3 minutes
   - Activate intrinsic foot muscles. Spread toes, press big toe down without curling
   - Foot strength = injury prevention (plantar fasciitis, etc.)

8. HIP 90/90 mobility: 2-3 min total
   - Hip rotational mobility - both running and climbing demand this

9. COUCH STRETCH: 90s each side
   - Hip flexor length. You sit a lot.

Total time: 30-40 min

weeks.focus: Field test week - validate threshold HR; introduce light tempo

workouts.name: 30-min Threshold Field Test
workouts.humanReadable:
FIELD TEST - calibrate your zones

WARM UP: 15min easy + 4x20s strides
MAIN: Run 30min ALL-OUT sustainable pace (race effort - imagine you'd be racing for 60min)
  - Find a flat, uninterrupted route
  - Record AVG HR for the full 30min = your LTHR
  - Avg pace ≈ threshold pace
COOL DOWN: 10min easy

Report back the result and I can update zones if they're off from estimates (175 LTHR, 8:00/mi).

workouts.description: Calibrate zones - 7 days post-illness recovery + 1 week of base building
workouts.humanReadable:
FIELD TEST - retesting after illness recovery

WARM UP: 15min easy + 4x20s strides
MAIN: Run 30min ALL-OUT sustainable pace (race effort - imagine you'd be racing for 60min)
  - Find a flat, uninterrupted route
  - Pace it like you'd race a 60-min event - don't blow up in the first 5min
  - GO HARDER than last time. The first test was during illness recovery.
  - Target: Avg HR climbing toward 175-178 by end, max HR 180+ on final minutes
COOL DOWN: 10min easy

Record:
  - Avg HR over the 30 min = your LTHR
  - Avg pace over the 30 min = your threshold pace

Report the result and I'll update zones + W6+ paces.

If you feel any lingering illness or your HR is again capped (max < 180), STOP and we'll use the data plus the May 18 effort to estimate zones conservatively.

Goal: Maximum pulling strength for climbing. This is your highest-priority lift session for climbing performance.

Build phase: 4 sets, 6-8 reps, RPE 7-8 (1-2 reps in reserve). Add weight when you hit top of rep range cleanly.

workouts.name: A: Lower Body Heavy
workouts.description: Lift A - Posterior chain + eccentric quad. Phase: build.
workouts.humanReadable:
LOWER BODY HEAVY - Posterior chain + eccentric quad
Goal: Hip drive for climbing, posterior chain power for running, eccentric quad for downhill (St. George prep)

Build phase: 4 sets, 6-8 reps, RPE 7-8 (1-2 reps in reserve). Add weight when you hit top of rep range cleanly.

WARM UP (8-10 min):
  - 5 min easy bike or rowing
  - World's greatest stretch x 5 each side
  - Glute bridge x 15 (activation)
  - Bodyweight squat x 10
  - Cossack squat x 5 each

MAIN LIFT:
1. TRAP BAR DEADLIFT (or Romanian Deadlift if no trap bar): 4x6-8
   - Drive hips back, neutral spine, push floor away
   - Climbing crossover: hip hinge = body tension on the wall

   - Front foot far enough that knee tracks over ankle

3. BARBELL HIP THRUST (or Glute Bridge with band): 3x8-10
   - Pause 1 sec at top, full glute squeeze

   - 3-second descent with DBs
   - DIRECT downhill marathon prep - eccentric quad load

   - Slow 3-sec descent, full stretch at bottom
   - Achilles + foot resilience

6. PALLOF PRESS (anti-rotation): 3x10 each side
   - Core resists rotation - critical for running form + climbing tension

Total time: 45-55 min

workouts.name: C: Upper Push + Core
workouts.description: Lift C - Antagonist + stability. Phase: build.
workouts.humanReadable:
UPPER PUSH + CORE - Antagonist + stability
Goal: Antagonist work for climbing (prevents overuse injuries), shoulder stability, anti-extension/rotation core.
This is NOT a chest day. We're balancing the pulling-dominant climbing/back work.

Build phase: 4 sets, 6-8 reps, RPE 7-8 (1-2 reps in reserve). Add weight when you hit top of rep range cleanly.

WARM UP (5 min):
  - Arm circles + band pass-throughs x 10
  - Cat-cow x 10
  - Push-up to down dog x 8
  - Plank shoulder taps x 10 each

MAIN LIFT:
1. PUSH-UP variations (decline → standard → diamond): 4x8-10 (decline or weighted)
   - Hollow body position, full ROM, controlled tempo
   - Rotate variations week-to-week to keep stimulus varied

2. DUMBBELL OVERHEAD PRESS (standing): 4x6-8 (heavier)
   - Brace core hard, no lumbar extension. This is core work too.
   - Strong overhead press = healthy shoulders for sustained climbing

3. Y-T-W RAISES (light DB or band, prone): 3x8 each letter
   - Mid/lower trap activation - the muscles that protect your shoulders during climbing pulls

4. BANDED PULL-APART: 3x15
   - High volume, light resistance. Pure shoulder health work.

5. PLANK (front): 3x60s
   - Hollow body engagement. Glutes squeezed. NO sagging.

6. SIDE PLANK: 2x30-45s EACH side
   - Lateral core stability for running stride + climbing tension

7. DEAD BUG (slow, controlled): 3x10 EACH side
   - Anti-extension. Lock low back to floor.

8. HOLLOW BODY HOLD: 3x30-40s
   - Total body tension. Climbing's #1 core position. Hands and heels OFF floor.

9. PALLOF PRESS HOLD: 2x20s each side
   - Anti-rotation. Climbing on overhangs requires this.

Total time: 35-45 min

🏁 BRYCE CANYON HALF MARATHON 🏁

  - High cadence (180+), short stride
  - Don't 'brake' - let gravity work but stay relaxed

This is your dress rehearsal for St. George downhill mechanics

workouts.name: A: Lower Body Heavy
workouts.description: Lift A - Posterior chain + eccentric quad. Phase: light.
workouts.humanReadable:
LOWER BODY HEAVY - Posterior chain + eccentric quad
Goal: Hip drive for climbing, posterior chain power for running, eccentric quad for downhill (St. George prep)

Deload week: 2 sets, 8 reps, RPE 5-6 (very controlled). Active recovery only.

WARM UP (8-10 min):
  - 5 min easy bike or rowing
  - World's greatest stretch x 5 each side
  - Glute bridge x 15 (activation)
  - Bodyweight squat x 10
  - Cossack squat x 5 each

MAIN LIFT:
1. TRAP BAR DEADLIFT (or Romanian Deadlift if no trap bar): 2x8 (light)
   - Drive hips back, neutral spine, push floor away
   - Climbing crossover: hip hinge = body tension on the wall

2. BULGARIAN SPLIT SQUAT (DB in each hand): 2x8 (light) EACH leg
   - Front foot far enough that knee tracks over ankle
   - 3-second descent, drive up through heel
   - The single best exercise for runners. Don't skip.

3. BARBELL HIP THRUST (or Glute Bridge with band): 2x10 (light)
   - Pause 1 sec at top, full glute squeeze
   - Posterior chain power = late-race pace maintenance

4. REVERSE LUNGE - SLOW ECCENTRIC: 2x8 (light) EACH leg
   - 3-second descent with DBs
   - DIRECT downhill marathon prep - eccentric quad load

5. SINGLE-LEG CALF RAISE (off step): 2x10 EACH
   - Slow 3-sec descent, full stretch at bottom
   - Achilles + foot resilience

6. PALLOF PRESS (anti-rotation): 3x10 each side
   - Core resists rotation - critical for running form + climbing tension

Total time: 45-55 min

workouts.humanReadable: Rest or light climb (technical only)

workouts.name: C: Upper Push + Core
workouts.description: Lift C - Antagonist + stability. Phase: light.
workouts.humanReadable:
UPPER PUSH + CORE - Antagonist + stability
Goal: Antagonist work for climbing (prevents overuse injuries), shoulder stability, anti-extension/rotation core.
This is NOT a chest day. We're balancing the pulling-dominant climbing/back work.

Deload week: 2 sets, 8 reps, RPE 5-6 (very controlled). Active recovery only.

WARM UP (5 min):
  - Arm circles + band pass-throughs x 10
  - Cat-cow x 10
  - Push-up to down dog x 8
  - Plank shoulder taps x 10 each

MAIN LIFT:
1. PUSH-UP variations (decline → standard → diamond): 2x8 (no failure)
   - Hollow body position, full ROM, controlled tempo
   - Rotate variations week-to-week to keep stimulus varied

2. DUMBBELL OVERHEAD PRESS (standing): 2x8 (light)
   - Brace core hard, no lumbar extension. This is core work too.
   - Strong overhead press = healthy shoulders for sustained climbing

3. Y-T-W RAISES (light DB or band, prone): 3x8 each letter
   - Mid/lower trap activation - the muscles that protect your shoulders during climbing pulls

4. BANDED PULL-APART: 3x15
   - High volume, light resistance. Pure shoulder health work.

5. PLANK (front): 3x30-45s
   - Hollow body engagement. Glutes squeezed. NO sagging.

6. SIDE PLANK: 2x30-45s EACH side
   - Lateral core stability for running stride + climbing tension

7. DEAD BUG (slow, controlled): 3x10 EACH side
   - Anti-extension. Lock low back to floor.

8. HOLLOW BODY HOLD: 3x20s
   - Total body tension. Climbing's #1 core position. Hands and heels OFF floor.

9. PALLOF PRESS HOLD: 2x20s each side
   - Anti-rotation. Climbing on overhangs requires this.

Total time: 35-45 min

workouts.name: B: Upper Pull (Climbing)
workouts.description: Lift B - Climbing-focused pulling. Phase: maintain.
workouts.humanReadable:
UPPER PULL - Climbing focus
Goal: Maximum pulling strength for climbing. This is your highest-priority lift session for climbing performance.

Maintain phase: 3 sets, 6-8 reps, RPE 7. Hold strength while running takes priority.

WARM UP (8 min):
  - 5 min light pulling (banded or jumping pull-ups easy)
  - Shoulder CARs x 5 each direction
  - Scap pull-ups x 10
  - Band pull-aparts x 15
  - Easy hangs (bar) 2x 15s

MAIN LIFT:
1. WEIGHTED PULL-UP (or strict bodyweight if not yet weighted): 3x4-6
   - Full ROM: chin clearly over bar, full elbow extension at bottom
   - Climbing's #1 strength predictor

2. ONE-ARM DUMBBELL ROW: 3x8 EACH
   - Pause at top, control descent. Lock shoulder blade down.

3. INVERTED ROW or TRX ROW: 3x10
   - Horizontal pulling balances vertical (pull-up)

4. HANGBOARD PROTOCOL: 5 sets max hang 7s on 20mm edge
   - 20mm edges, half-crimp or open-hand
   - 90 sec rest between hangs
   - SKIP if fingers feel sore from climbing this week

5. FACE PULL: 3x12-15
   - External rotation + scap retraction = healthy shoulders for high-volume climbing

6. HAMMER CURL (forearm flexor + brachialis): 2x10
   - Forearm endurance for sustained crimping. KEEP this brief - not the goal.

7. WRIST CURL + WRIST EXTENSION: 2x12 each
   - Forearm extensor balance prevents elbow tendinopathy from climbing

8. DEAD HANG (open-hand): 3x max time (target 45s+)
   - Grip endurance + scap stability

Total time: 50-60 min

workouts.name: C: Upper Push + Core
workouts.description: Lift C - Antagonist + stability. Phase: peak.
workouts.humanReadable:
UPPER PUSH + CORE - Antagonist + stability
Goal: Antagonist work for climbing (prevents overuse injuries), shoulder stability, anti-extension/rotation core.
This is NOT a chest day. We're balancing the pulling-dominant climbing/back work.

Peak phase: 4 sets, 5-6 reps, RPE 8 (1 rep in reserve). Heavy compound work + plyometrics for downhill prep.

WARM UP (5 min):
  - Arm circles + band pass-throughs x 10
  - Cat-cow x 10
  - Push-up to down dog x 8
  - Plank shoulder taps x 10 each

MAIN LIFT:
1. PUSH-UP variations (decline → standard → diamond): 4x6-8 (weighted/decline)
   - Hollow body position, full ROM, controlled tempo
   - Rotate variations week-to-week to keep stimulus varied

2. DUMBBELL OVERHEAD PRESS (standing): 4x5-6 (heavy)
   - Brace core hard, no lumbar extension. This is core work too.
   - Strong overhead press = healthy shoulders for sustained climbing

3. Y-T-W RAISES (light DB or band, prone): 3x8 each letter
   - Mid/lower trap activation - the muscles that protect your shoulders during climbing pulls

4. BANDED PULL-APART: 3x15
   - High volume, light resistance. Pure shoulder health work.

5. PLANK (front): 3x60s
   - Hollow body engagement. Glutes squeezed. NO sagging.

6. SIDE PLANK: 2x30-45s EACH side
   - Lateral core stability for running stride + climbing tension

7. DEAD BUG (slow, controlled): 3x10 EACH side
   - Anti-extension. Lock low back to floor.

workouts.name: D: Lower Power + Foot
workouts.description: Lift D - Plyometric + injury prevention. Phase: peak.
workouts.humanReadable:
LOWER POWER + FOOT/STABILITY - Light, plyometric, prevention
Goal: Plyometric power for running economy + foot/calf injury prevention. KEEP THIS LIGHT.
You did your heavy lower work on Wed; this is pure quality movement, not loading.

Peak phase: 4 sets, 5-6 reps, RPE 8 (1 rep in reserve). Heavy compound work + plyometrics for downhill prep.

WARM UP (5 min):
  - Easy jog 5 min OR jump rope
  - Ankle CARs x 10 each direction
  - Calf stretch + Soleus stretch (knee-bent) 30s each
  - A-skips x 20m, B-skips x 20m

MAIN MOVEMENT (POWER):
1. POGO HOPS (low, springy): 3x30s
   - Stay on the ball of foot, minimal ground contact
   - Building tendon stiffness = running economy

2. DEPTH JUMP (24-inch box): 3x5 (eccentric absorption + reactive jump - DOWNHILL PREP)

3. SINGLE-LEG BOUNDING (controlled): 3x10 EACH (ONLY if form is clean - skip if fatigued)

PREVENTION & STABILITY:
4. SINGLE-LEG GLUTE BRIDGE: 3x10 EACH leg
   - 1-second pause at top
   - Often-neglected glute med work

5. TIBIALIS RAISE (back to wall, lift toes up): 3x15 EACH leg
   - The anterior shin muscle prevents shin splints. Critical for high-mileage weeks.

6. SINGLE-LEG CALF RAISE (slow, off step): 3x12 EACH
   - 3-sec eccentric. Achilles resilience.

7. TOE YOGA / SHORT FOOT: 2-3 minutes
   - Activate intrinsic foot muscles. Spread toes, press big toe down without curling
   - Foot strength = injury prevention (plantar fasciitis, etc.)

8. HIP 90/90 mobility: 2-3 min total
   - Hip rotational mobility - both running and climbing demand this

9. COUCH STRETCH: 90s each side
   - Hip flexor length. You sit a lot.

Total time: 30-40 min

9. COUCH STRETCH: 90s each side

workouts.name: B: Upper Pull (Climbing)
workouts.description: Lift B - Climbing-focused pulling. Phase: maintain.
workouts.humanReadable:
UPPER PULL - Climbing focus
Goal: Maximum pulling strength for climbing. This is your highest-priority lift session for climbing performance.

Maintain phase: 3 sets, 6-8 reps, RPE 7. Hold strength while running takes priority.

WARM UP (8 min):
  - 5 min light pulling (banded or jumping pull-ups easy)
  - Shoulder CARs x 5 each direction
  - Scap pull-ups x 10
  - Band pull-aparts x 15
  - Easy hangs (bar) 2x 15s

MAIN LIFT:
1. WEIGHTED PULL-UP (or strict bodyweight if not yet weighted): 3x4-6
   - Full ROM: chin clearly over bar, full elbow extension at bottom
   - Climbing's #1 strength predictor

2. ONE-ARM DUMBBELL ROW: 3x8 EACH
   - Pause at top, control descent. Lock shoulder blade down.

3. INVERTED ROW or TRX ROW: 3x10
   - Horizontal pulling balances vertical (pull-up)

4. HANGBOARD PROTOCOL: 5 sets max hang 7s on 20mm edge
   - 20mm edges, half-crimp or open-hand
   - 90 sec rest between hangs
   - SKIP if fingers feel sore from climbing this week

5. FACE PULL: 3x12-15
   - External rotation + scap retraction = healthy shoulders for high-volume climbing

6. HAMMER CURL (forearm flexor + brachialis): 2x10
   - Forearm endurance for sustained crimping. KEEP this brief - not the goal.

7. WRIST CURL + WRIST EXTENSION: 2x12 each
   - Forearm extensor balance prevents elbow tendinopathy from climbing

8. DEAD HANG (open-hand): 3x max time (target 45s+)
   - Grip endurance + scap stability

Total time: 50-60 min

workouts.description: Lift A - Posterior chain + eccentric quad. Phase: maintain.
workouts.humanReadable:
LOWER BODY HEAVY - Posterior chain + eccentric quad
Goal: Hip drive for climbing, posterior chain power for running, eccentric quad for downhill (St. George prep)

Maintain phase: 3 sets, 6-8 reps, RPE 7. Hold strength while running takes priority.

WARM UP (8-10 min):
  - 5 min easy bike or rowing
  - World's greatest stretch x 5 each side
  - Glute bridge x 15 (activation)
  - Bodyweight squat x 10
  - Cossack squat x 5 each

MAIN LIFT:
1. TRAP BAR DEADLIFT (or Romanian Deadlift if no trap bar): 3x6-8
   - Drive hips back, neutral spine, push floor away
   - Climbing crossover: hip hinge = body tension on the wall

2. BULGARIAN SPLIT SQUAT (DB in each hand): 3x6-8 EACH leg
   - Front foot far enough that knee tracks over ankle
   - 3-second descent, drive up through heel
   - The single best exercise for runners. Don't skip.

3. BARBELL HIP THRUST (or Glute Bridge with band): 3x8-10
   - Pause 1 sec at top, full glute squeeze
   - Posterior chain power = late-race pace maintenance

4. REVERSE LUNGE - SLOW ECCENTRIC: 3x6-8 EACH leg
   - 3-second descent with DBs
   - DIRECT downhill marathon prep - eccentric quad load

5. SINGLE-LEG CALF RAISE (off step): 3x10-12 EACH
   - Slow 3-sec descent, full stretch at bottom
   - Achilles + foot resilience

6. PALLOF PRESS (anti-rotation): 3x10 each side
   - Core resists rotation - critical for running form + climbing tension

Total time: 45-55 min

workouts.name: C: Upper Push + Core
workouts.description: Lift C - Antagonist + stability. Phase: maintain.
workouts.humanReadable:
UPPER PUSH + CORE - Antagonist + stability
Goal: Antagonist work for climbing (prevents overuse injuries), shoulder stability, anti-extension/rotation core.
This is NOT a chest day. We're balancing the pulling-dominant climbing/back work.

Maintain phase: 3 sets, 6-8 reps, RPE 7. Hold strength while running takes priority.

WARM UP (5 min):
  - Arm circles + band pass-throughs x 10
  - Cat-cow x 10
  - Push-up to down dog x 8
  - Plank shoulder taps x 10 each

MAIN LIFT:
1. PUSH-UP variations (decline → standard → diamond): 3x8-10
   - Hollow body position, full ROM, controlled tempo
   - Rotate variations week-to-week to keep stimulus varied

2. DUMBBELL OVERHEAD PRESS (standing): 3x6-8
   - Brace core hard, no lumbar extension. This is core work too.
   - Strong overhead press = healthy shoulders for sustained climbing

3. Y-T-W RAISES (light DB or band, prone): 3x8 each letter
   - Mid/lower trap activation - the muscles that protect your shoulders during climbing pulls

4. BANDED PULL-APART: 3x15
   - High volume, light resistance. Pure shoulder health work.

5. PLANK (front): 3x60s
   - Hollow body engagement. Glutes squeezed. NO sagging.

6. SIDE PLANK: 2x30-45s EACH side
   - Lateral core stability for running stride + climbing tension

7. DEAD BUG (slow, controlled): 3x10 EACH side
   - Anti-extension. Lock low back to floor.

8. HOLLOW BODY HOLD: 3x30s
   - Total body tension. Climbing's #1 core position. Hands and heels OFF floor.

9. PALLOF PRESS HOLD: 2x20s each side
   - Anti-rotation. Climbing on overhangs requires this.

Total time: 35-45 min

workouts.name: 🏁 ST. GEORGE MARATHON 🏁
workouts.humanReadable:
🏁 ST. GEORGE MARATHON - YOUR A-RACE 🏁

GOAL TIERS:
  A: Sub-3:45 (8:35/mi) - target goal
  B: Sub-3:55 (8:58/mi) - solid PR
  C: Sub-4:06 (9:23/mi) - any PR over Big Sur

RACE MORNING:
  - 4:00 AM: Wake up. Coffee, water, light breakfast (3hr before start)
    - 100-150g carbs (oatmeal + banana + bagel + honey)
  - 5:00 AM: Bus to start at Pine Valley (5,200ft elevation, COLD - bring throwaway clothes)
  - 6:30 AM: Last bathroom, last sip of water
  - 6:45 AM: Race kit on, half gel + 8oz water
  - 7:00 AM: GUN

PACING - KEY TO ST. GEORGE SUCCESS:
  - Mi 1-5 (downhill from Pine Valley): 8:50-9:05 - GO SLOWER THAN GOAL. The descent is steep & runnable. Holding back here saves your quads for mile 18+
  - Mi 6-8 (Veyo Hill - the only major climb): power hike if needed, HR <175. Don't go in the red here.
  - Mi 9-13 (rolling, mostly down): settle into 8:30-8:40
  - Mi 14-19 (sustained gentle descent): 8:25-8:35 - this is where you make time
  - Mi 20-26.2 (mostly flat into St. George): 8:30-8:45 - HOLD FORM, this is where everyone falls apart. You won't, because your quads are intact from holding back early.

QUAD MANAGEMENT (the difference between 3:45 and 4:30):
  - Slight forward lean from ankles (not waist)
  - Quick cadence 180+, short stride
  - Don't "brake" - let gravity pull, stay relaxed
  - If quads start to scream by mi 18, you went too fast early

FUEL/HYDRATION:
  - Gel every 4 miles (mi 4, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24)
  - At least 2 caffeine gels (mi 16 and mi 20)
  - Sports drink at every aid station (~every 1.5mi)
  - Total target: 60-90g carbs/hr

MENTAL CHECKPOINTS:
  - Mi 5: "How easy am I going?" - it should feel TOO easy
  - Mi 13: "Halfway. Do I feel fresh?" - if yes, hold pace
  - Mi 18: "How are the quads?" - if good, push to MP. If sore, hold conservative
  - Mi 22: "4 miles to go. Now I race."

YOU TRAINED FOR THIS. Trust the plan. Trust your fitness. Run YOUR race.

event.name: St. George Marathon
event.elevation: Net -2,560 ft from Pine Valley (5,200 ft) to St. George (2,800 ft)
event.courseNotes: Predominantly downhill with one major climb (Veyo Hill, mi 6-8). Course favors fast times but DESTROYS quads if pacing is wrong

tunup.name: Bryce Canyon Half Marathon
tunup.purpose: Fitness test, downhill mechanics practice, dress rehearsal for nutrition/pacing

halfMarathon.strategy: Hold back first 3mi on descent (8:35-8:45). Settle 4-9 (8:25-8:35). Push final 5K (8:15-8:25)

marathon.strategy: Conservative first 5mi (8:50-9:05). Power hike Veyo if needed. Settle 9-13 (8:30-8:40). Make time 14-19 (8:25-8:35). HOLD form 20-26 (8:30-8:45)
marathon.hrCeiling: 163 (Z3 ceiling) for first 18 miles, then up to 170 final 8 miles

nutrition.preRace: 3 hours before: 100-150g carbs (oatmeal, banana, bagel + honey) + coffee + water. 30min before: half gel + sip drink

products: Sports drink at all aid stations
during.schedule: Gel every 4 miles + sips of sports drink at each aid station (~every 1.5mi)

nutrition.carbLoad: Begin Thursday before race: 8-10g carbs per kg body weight. Friday: same. Saturday morning: 100-150g pre-race
nutrition.notes: PRACTICE this exact protocol on the 18-mi (W16), 20-mi (W17 & W19), and 22-mi (W20) long runs. Do not try anything new on race day.

taper.intensityRetention: Maintain weekly tempo + threshold touch through W22 Tuesday
taper.notes: Most athletes under-taper. Trust the fitness. You won't lose anything by resting more, but you can lose plenty by training too much.

downhillSpecific.training: Long runs in W14-W20 should include 3-6mi of sustained descent. This builds eccentric quad strength (the type that breaks down on St. George's descent).
downhillSpecific.raceDay: First 5 miles are critical - going too fast on the early descent is the #1 reason runners blow up at St. George. Your watch will say you're 30-45sec/mi behind goal pace - THAT IS CORRECT. You'll make it back.
downhillSpecific.shoes: Prioritize shoes with cushioning over plates. Race shoes that worked on the 22-mile dress rehearsal are the only shoes you race in.

raceStrategy.weatherContingency: St. George early October typical: 50°F start, 75°F finish. If hot (>80°F): adjust goal +2-4 min and increase fluid to 28oz/hr. If cold (<45°F): wear gloves & throwaway shirt at start

strengthProgram.philosophy: Climbing-first, running-injury-prevention second, aesthetic muscle is not a goal. Pulling-dominant for climbing performance, posterior chain + eccentric quad for running and St. George downhill, antagonist work to keep shoulders/elbows healthy under climbing volume.

weeklyStructure.Mon: AM SPEED Run + PM Lift B (Upper Pull - climbing focus)
weeklyStructure.Tue: Rest or light climb (true recovery day)
weeklyStructure.Wed: AM Easy Run + PM Lift A (Lower Heavy - posterior chain)
weeklyStructure.Thu: AM TEMPO Run + PM Lift C (Upper Push + Core)
weeklyStructure.Sun: AM Climb (moderate) + PM Lift D (Lower Power - light)

rules: Lift A (Lower Heavy) is on WED to leave 72 hours before Sat long run
Lift B (Upper Pull) is on MON because pulling doesn't fatigue legs
Lift C (Upper Push) is on THU because no leg fatigue interferes with Sat long run
Lift D (Lower Power) is on SUN, kept LIGHT, 48 hours before next Tue quality run
If something has to give, drop Lift D (Sun) first, then Lift C (Thu)
Always do strength AFTER running on the same day, separated by 4-6 hours minimum
Skip all lifting in the 5 days before a race (W10 from Tue, W22 from Tue)
scheduleNotes: Mon speed run + Mon PM upper pull lift = climbing-focused upper work on your hardest leg day
Tue is a TRUE recovery day - rest or technical/easy climbing only
Wed Lower Heavy lift comes before Thu Tempo - legs will feel mildly tired Thu morning. This is by design and adapts within 2-3 weeks. If Thu tempos feel impossible to hit, swap Lift A to Tue PM.
Thu Tempo + Sat Long Run = 1 day rest (Fri). Honor the Fri rest day - no leg lifting Friday.
Sun light lower power lift is 48 hours before Mon speed - plenty of recovery
